§6Bakelite§f

§aAn alternative plastic made entirely from oil fluids — no coal or fluorite needed!§f

Bakelite (formally polyoxybenzylmethyleneglycolanhydride) is an alternative plastic produced from §bAromatic Hydrocarbons§f and §bPetroleum Gas§f in the §bChemical Plant§f. It is fully interchangeable with Polymer in virtually all recipes, and is far easier to mass-produce since it requires only fluid inputs.

§6Production§f:
§b• Chemical Plant§f: §e500mB§f Aromatic Hydrocarbons + §e500mB§f Petroleum Gas → §e1×§f Bakelite Bar (requires Chemistry Template).
§b• Laboratory Glassware§f (alt): Combine Hazardous Material Tank of Aromatic HC + Gas Tank of Petroleum Gas → §e2×§f Bakelite Powder, then smelt into bars.

§6Uses§f:
§b• Insulators§f: §e1×§f Bakelite Bar → §e8×§f Insulators (crafting table), essential for circuits.
§b• Industrial Boiler§f: Required for crafting high-tier boilers.
§b• Polymer Substitute§f: Bakelite replaces Polymer in every crafting recipe.

§eTip§f: Bakelite is strictly better than Polymer for mass production — it requires no §bcoal powder§f or §bfluorite§f, only oil products that can be continuously refined. Set up a dedicated Chemical Plant running Aromatic HC + Petroleum Gas for all your plastic needs!
